name: Janggi
author: AGP (traditional Korean Chess)
description: Janggi (Korean Chess) on a 9x10 board, the Korean sibling of Xiangqi. Generals and guards move orthogonally or along the palace diagonals and stay in the palace; horses and elephants leap (1+1 and 1+2) and can be blocked along their path; cannons jump exactly one screen but may neither jump over, capture, nor use another cannon as a screen; soldiers step forward or sideways and may follow the palace diagonals inside the enemy palace; elephants and soldiers cross freely (no river). Checkmate or stalemate the enemy general to win. Cho moves first.
